248.reason: [When encountering a single '&', the current character has already been consumed by the outer loop (c = line.charAt(idx++)), so the error should be reported immediately without trying to read the next character. However, more critically, after detecting an error, the program returns early but does not stop processing further lines in some cases; however, the main issue here is that when idx is at the end of the line, line.charAt(idx++) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the current character has already been consumed by the outer loop (c = line.charAt(idx++)), so the error should be reported immediately without trying to read the next character. However, more critically, after detecting an error, the program returns early but does not stop processing further lines in some cases; however, the main issue here is that when idx is at the end of the line, line.charAt(idx++) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the code consumes the next character unconditionally via `line.charAt(idx++)`, which may cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException if '&' is the last character on the line. Additionally, even if not at end-of-line, it fails to check whether the next character actually exists before accessing it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the current character pointer has already advanced past the next character, but if the next character does not exist (i.e., idx is beyond the line length), line.charAt(idx++) will throw a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException. Additionally, even if safe, the code fails to handle the case where '&' appears at the end of a line correctly—it should detect the error but may crash before doing so.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the code consumes the next character unconditionally via `line.charAt(idx++)`, which may cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException if '&' is the last character on the line. Additionally, even if safe, it fails to properly handle the case where the next character exists but is not '&'; it should report the error without consuming the next character as part of the '&'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the current character has already been consumed by idx++, but the error handling does not account for the fact that the second character might be beyond the string length (i.e., idx may exceed line.length()), leading to StringIndexOutOfBoundsException or incorrect error reporting. Additionally, after reading the next char unconditionally with line.charAt(idx++), if idx was already at line.length(), this causes an exception.]

261.reason: [Same issue as above: if '|' is the last character in the line, line.charAt(idx++) will cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException because idx is already equal to line.length().]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: if '|' is the last character in the line, line.charAt(idx++) will cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException because idx is already equal to line.length().]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: `line.charAt(idx++)` is called without verifying that `idx < line.length()`, which can lead to StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when '|' is the last character in the line.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as with '&': if '|' is the last character in the line, line.charAt(idx++) will cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException before the error can be properly reported. The code does not check bounds before accessing line.charAt(idx++).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: `line.charAt(idx++)` is called without checking if `idx < line.length()`, risking an IndexOutOfBoundsException when '|' is the last character. Also, the single '|' should trigger error 'a' without consuming the next character, but the current logic advances `idx` before validating, leading to incorrect parsing or crash.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: when c is '|' and it's the last character in the line, line.charAt(idx++) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException because idx equals line.length() before the increment, and accessing charAt(line.length()) is invalid. The code fails to check bounds before accessing the next character.]
